founder.title: The founder of scouting
founder.subtitle: Robert Stephenson Smyth Baden-Powell
founder.intro: The founder of the worldwide scout movement, whose ideas still guide scouts across the globe today.
founder.photoCaption: B-P meets the Cub Scouts at the 1st International Jamboree at Olympia, 1920.

timeline.text: On 22 February, Robert Stephenson Smyth Baden-Powell, founder of world scouting, was born. His father, H. G. Baden-Powell, was a professor at Oxford University and a member of the Royal Society, author of many works on philosophy and theology.

He began learning seamanship aboard the ship "Conway". He never parted from the ship, even on holidays — sailing the sea with his brothers. He quickly learned to handle a boat, camp, cook and carry out orders, gaining the experience of a naturalist and scout.
He passed the entrance exams to Charterhouse School.
Charterhouse moved to Godalming. In the dense woods nearby Robert learned to stalk, track, observe birds and animals, pitch a tent and use a hatchet.
He joined the British Army and went to fight in India.

During the siege of Mafeking he became known for organising volunteer boys — cadets — who carried out signalling duties with courage and responsibility. This was the beginning of the idea of scouting.
Queen Victoria granted him the rank of Major-General.
He was awarded the title of Lord.
He held the first scout camp on Brownsea Island — the date considered the official beginning of scouting.
He published the book "Scouting for Boys".
He retired from the army and devoted himself to scouting.
He set off on a journey around the world to study scouting in different countries. On the trip he met Olave St. Clair Soames, whom he married on returning to England.
On 17 August, Baden-Powell and Olave, patroness of the Girl Guides, arrived in Palanga with their family and 650 English scout and guide leaders.
In failing health he travelled to Africa (Kenya).
He died on 8 January and is buried in Africa.

history.title: History
intro: Scouts (from English "scout") form a voluntary, non-formal educational organisation for children and young people.
Following the ideas of founder Robert Baden-Powell (1857–1941), scouts aim to raise their members as honest, noble-spirited, strong-charactered, healthy, conscientious people and useful citizens.
Worldwide, the scout movement has more than 28 million scouts of all ages from 216 countries and regions. Across its whole history, over 580 million people have belonged to it.
mottos: Scout motto — "For God, Homeland and neighbour"
Sea Scout motto — "For God, Homeland, the Sea and neighbour"
history.timelineTitle: History of the Lithuanian Sea Scouts

paragraphs: In Africa, during the Boer War, Great Britain fought the Orange Free State and the Transvaal. At one point the Boers besieged the small town of Mafeking, whose garrison was led by Colonel Robert Baden-Powell. He gathered a company of volunteers from the town's boys, giving them various tasks: carrying letters and orders, bringing ammunition and food, transporting the wounded, scouting and drawing maps. The siege lasted half a year.

Back in England, now a general, Baden-Powell set out to occupy children with useful and interesting activity. In 1907 he held the first scout camp with twenty boys, and described his system of youth education — scouting — in the 1908 book "Scouting for Boys".

Girls soon took an interest too — guide groups began forming in 1909–1910. From England the movement spread to the colonies and other countries. After the First World War the military element was dropped from scouting.
The first scout unit in Lithuania was established. The pioneer of scouting in Lithuania was Petras Jurgėla-Jurgelevičius.
The Lithuanian Scout Association (LSA), uniting all Lithuanian scouts, and the Lithuanian Sea Scout Union were founded.

The Lithuanian Sea Scout Union was established in Kaunas on 12 March 1922, after the first patrol was organised at the "Aušra" gymnasium, and on 19 April the first troop was formed from the "Otters" and "Beavers" patrols. The troop was sponsored by the Lithuanian Yacht Club in Kaunas.
In 1923 a Guides division was established. In 1924 the association was registered with WOSM; that same year saw the historic sea scout voyage from Kaunas to Klaipėda.
In 1925 the association became the Lithuanian Scout Brotherhood. The sea scouts operated as a separate organisation (led by Petras Jurgėla), rejoining the Lithuanian Scout Union in 1927.
In 1929 Povilas Dagys and Mykolas Frankas founded the first sea scout unit in Biržai. In 1930 the organisation was named the Lithuanian Scout Union.
In 1933 Lord Robert Baden-Powell visited the Lithuanian scouts. In 1935 a parade featured 115 sea scout kayaks. By 1936 the inland-water routes covered by sea scouts totalled 10,000 kilometres.

After the Soviet occupation on 15 June 1940 the organisation's activity was halted and continued in exile. In 1988 the scouting movement was reborn — sea scouts first formed in Kaunas and Klaipėda. The restoration congress took place on 29 April 1989.
In 1991 the "Žemaitija Scout Organisation" was founded. In 1995 the former LSS split into two organisations — the Lithuanian Scout Union (LSS) and Lithuanian Scouting (LS).
On 25 July 1997 Lithuanian Scouting became a member of the World Organization of the Scout Movement (WOSM). In 2001 Lithuanian Sea Scouting was founded.

maritime.title: The path of the Lithuanian sea scouts
maritime.intro: A fuller history of sea scouting — from the early interwar years to the present day and the diaspora.

chapters.title: The beginnings and growth of the sea scouts in Lithuania
paragraphs: For several years the sea scouts were sponsored by the so-called Lithuanian Yacht Club, established in Kaunas in 1921 — an organisation that united enthusiasts of every kind of water sport (not only yachting). From those days to the present the sea scouts have used an inherited flag — the flag of the former Yacht Club (a symmetrical blue cross on a white field, with the national tricolour in the upper quarter by the staff) with the sea scout emblem — a fleur-de-lis with an anchor — added opposite it in the lower quarter (by the rules of heraldry the emblem should be in the lower quarter by the staff). Incidentally, the former flag of that yacht club has in our day become the flag of the Lithuanian Naval Force.

At the start of 1922 the "Rimgaudas" troop in Kaunas had 6 patrols. The troop leader's assistant, senior patrol leader Julius Jurgėla Jurgelevičius (aged 16), founded a water scout patrol. The start was active in scoutcraft and in preparing for water activities and for service in the Lithuanian navy. Soon a second patrol formed, and on 12 March 1922 J. Jurgėla, with both patrols, broke away from the "Rimgaudas" troop and created a new sea scout troop under the name "Algimantas". The first patrols were named the "Otters" and the "Beavers". The troop staff chose Lithuanian nautical names, set the programme of sea scout proficiency grades, and designed a badge and a uniform. From 1925, having received no confirmation from the LSS, the Lithuanian sea scouts operated as a separate, independent organisation led by Petras Jurgėla, but in 1927 they once again merged with the united Lithuanian Scout Union.

Historic for the Lithuanian sea scouts was the voyage from Kaunas to Klaipėda in the spring of 1924 in two small sailing boats — the "Gulbė" (Swan), captained by Kostas Jurgėla, and the "Ruonis" (Seal), captained by Petras Labanauskas (who later finished his studies in Italy, became a naval officer and commanded the training warship "Prezidentas Smetona"). The result of the voyage was the founding of the first sea scout troop branch in Klaipėda, which soon became the spiritual centre of gravity of our entire sea scout movement. Later sea scout units sprang up across the country — in Panevėžys, Mažeikiai, Telšiai, Biržai, Ukmergė, Tauragė, Alytus, Jurbarkas, Kėdainiai, Marijampolė, Rokiškis, Utena, even in such a remote little town as Viekšniai on the Venta. New units formed in the cradle of sea scouting, Kaunas, and there and in Klaipėda larger formations took shape — the sea scout units.

chapters.title: Along the waterways of old Lithuania
paragraphs: In 1930 the first girl sea scout crew, the "Gulbės" (Swans), was established in Mažeikiai. The founding of the girl sea scouts, however, is dated to 16 February 1936, when the "Birutės" crew was established. The activity of the girl sea scouts spread less rapidly than that of the brothers. By 1940 there were 100 girl sea scouts, 5 crews and 2 boats.

A great event not only for the scouts but for the whole of Lithuanian society was the visit of the movement's founder and ideologue R. Baden-Powell with his wife — the organiser of the girls' scout movement — and an escort of 650 English scouts. In the summer of 1933 this Baden-Powell mission visited, aboard the ship "Calgaric", every state of the Baltic Sea except Hitler's Germany and Stalin's Russia. For several days the large passenger ship adorned the port of Klaipėda.

In those days the sea scouts widely cultivated journeys by boat and kayak on inland waters. In 1934, for the first Sea Day, which drew tens of thousands of guests to Klaipėda, 60 sea scout kayaks gathered from every corner of Lithuania. The expedition was led by the noted geographer and water-travel enthusiast Prof. S. Kolupaila. It is worth noting that the Sea Day, then solemnly marked across all Lithuania, was understood not as just another occasion to make merry, forget oneself and feast, but as a meaningful patriotic festival; it had no official beer, as it does now, but in return it had its lofty national idea.

At the parade held in 1935 on the occasion of the World Lithuanian Congress, as many as 115 sea scout kayaks took part, and before that 82 kayaks had sailed the rivers along the administrative line of the occupied Vilnius region. By 1936 the inland-water routes covered by the sea scouts amounted to 10,000 kilometres in all.

Many sea scouts from all over Lithuania attended the sailing school operating in Smiltynė, Klaipėda, in the summers of 1936 and 1937. The Biržai sea scouts took up the building of sailing boats themselves, building them not only for themselves but for other sea scout units too.

In the summer of 1939 a group of Biržai sea scouts, in a small sailing boat they had built themselves, reached the Baltic Sea by river through Latvia and sailed along the coast as far as Palanga. It was a symbolic expedition by which the young people sought to show that Lithuania, having lost Klaipėda in the spring of that year, would not give up its maritime aspirations. As the port of Šventoji was urgently being enlarged and adapted for overseas shipping as an alternative to the Klaipėda port seized by Hitler, and a new railway branch was being laid, the sea scouts took an active part in the youth working parties. These were no empty efforts: by 1940 the first sea merchant ship from Holland had already sailed into the port of Šventoji.

figures.caption: It was in this boat of their own making that the Biržai sea scouts sailed to Palanga by river and sea in 1939.

chapters.title: The sea scouts of Lithuania Minor
paragraphs: From the spring of 1924 in Klaipėda, encouraged by the Kaunas men who had sailed in and authorised by an official letter signed by the commodore of the Lithuanian Yacht Club and the chief scoutmaster, Martynas Brakas gathered the sea scouts — the youngest brother of Adomas Brakas, the noted public figure and artist of Lithuania Minor (both of them died far from their Homeland: Adomas, having withdrawn from Klaipėda to Kaunas in the spring of 1939 to escape the vengeance of the Hitlerites, found himself in Siberia within a year and a half and died in the Biysk concentration camp in 1942; Martynas had to emigrate and died in the USA after the rebirth of independent Lithuania).

The young people of Lithuania Minor, fired by the ideas of a maritime Lithuania and of scouting, were supported by the head of the Klaipėda port authority, an old sea dog, Captain Liudas Stulpinas; he gave them the use of a large rowing-and-sailing harbour training boat and part of the premises in Kopgalis fortress. The patriotically minded Klaipėda sea scouts wished to bear a Lithuanian name too — on the advice of Vydūnas himself they chose the name budžiai (from the scout sub-motto "Be prepared!").

chapters.title: The sea scouts of present-day Lithuania
paragraphs: The budžiai, having learned the basics of seamanship in the harbour waters, were soon fortunate enough to become the owners of a real seagoing sailing ship. This was the "Malaya", a Danzig spirit-smugglers' vessel caught by the border guards in Lithuanian waters off Palanga and later confiscated by the court. For almost a year the sturdy, handsome but greatly neglected oak cutter — of 24 tonnes' displacement and 80 square metres of sail — had stood by the customs quay, and the sea scouts acquired it at auction with a subsidy from the Lithuanian Women's Society for the support of a national fleet. Having repaired, fitted out and repainted the ship with their own hands and given it the name "Budys", on 1 October 1926 they set out on their first voyage to Liepāja. This was the beginning of Lithuanian sporting and training sailing: such sailing had been brought to Klaipėda in the 19th century by English merchants living there, and the sailing club "Memeler Segelverein", founded in 1884, was made up mostly of foreigners or wealthy local Germans. (Incidentally, the first Lithuanian foreign-trade ships to sail under the Lithuanian flag, even before Klaipėda was regained, were the cargo sailing ships "Jūratė" and "Kastytis".)

The sea scouts travelled widely in the "Budys", not disdaining the Curonian Lagoon either, and visited the ports of almost all the Baltic Sea countries. On 15 July 1933 a storm dashed the "Budys", as it was setting out to sea, against the northern mole of the Klaipėda entrance, and three crew members perished. Later the sea scouts acquired three small seagoing yachts of the then-popular "Hai" class (the first two named "Budys I" and "Budys II") and a larger one, the "Baltoji lelija" (White Lily), with which they took part in — and even won — sailing races in Lithuania and abroad.

In those days Lithuania strove most vigorously to establish itself at sea, though it had begun to build its maritime potential from the most meagre of beginnings; the sea scouts were a living source of future strength. Almost 70 per cent of the personnel of the Lithuanian merchant and naval fleet had come from the sea scouts.

Close to the sea both literally and figuratively was the youth organisation of the Lithuanians of the Klaipėda region — Santara, or simply Santara — from 1924 an independent offshoot of the old Union of Prussian Lithuanian Societies that had existed even before the First World War. Santara, with over 4,000 members, gathered young people for cultural and community work, set up choirs, drama circles and travelling libraries, developed youth sport and had a weekly slot on Klaipėda radio. In 1934, when Santara drew closer to the "Young Lithuania" union, a maritime section was established. Three of Santara's yachts, together with the boats of the yacht club and the sea scouts, took part successfully in maritime regattas.

All this youth work, which had already borne fine fruit, was destroyed in 1940 by the Soviet occupation. For Lithuanians the sea became scarcely accessible for a long time, and until the time of Sąjūdis it was not even permitted to mention the scout name in public.

The Sąjūdis movement of national rebirth freed people from fear, and then also from the total control of the Soviet authorities. Conditions arose for the rebirth of political, community, religious and national organisations independent of the regime's will — both new ones and those with old, not yet wholly forgotten traditions. This process was spontaneous, and chance people with selfish aims, and at times even hostile infiltrated elements, soon began attaching themselves to it.

In 1988 the scouting movement was reborn — sadly not as one whole, splitting apart at once. The sea scouts first began to form in Kaunas and Klaipėda. In Klaipėda a sea scout unit existed for a year or two, belonging to the Lithuanian division of a separate scouting current — the Catholic Scouts of Europe, or Euroscouts (with their centre in Paris). The Klaipėda scouts cultivated sailing, although they had no yachts of their own, and did take part in races at sea, winning a place in the "Budys" memorial regatta held annually since 1987. After the Euroscout group broke up, almost nothing was heard of sea scout activity in Klaipėda for nearly a decade; now they seem to be reviving here again and maintain contact with the maritime history and culture club named after the "Budys", founded in 1988.

The Kaunas sea scouts (who began their activity within the Lithuanian Scout Union) took over part of the equipment from the liquidated sea club of the Soviet semi-military DOSAAF organisation, and acquired a modest base of sorts by the Kaunas Sea, so they had relatively decent conditions in which to work.

In the summer of 1991 a group of Kaunas scouts more or less repeated the pioneering expedition of their predecessors of the 1920s — in two training boats ("yals") and seven kayaks they set off down the Nemunas towards the coast, reached Nida, met the Klaipėda Euroscouts there, camped together and tidied up the National Park's surroundings. Kaunas is currently home to the "Divytis" and "Nemunas" units.

In Vilnius an attempt was made to found the first sea scout unit in the spring of 1989 at the S. Nėris secondary school, but it withered away that same year; this activity really began in 1993, when the first camp for newcomers was held in the summer on an island in Lake Gulbinas, followed by an excursion to Klaipėda, a visit aboard Lithuanian warships and a hike along the Neris to Kaunas. Since then the "Vidgautas" unit has operated in Vilnius.

Until 2013 the "Vėtra" unit operated in Panevėžys. The "Šešupė" unit currently operates in Marijampolė. A few units operate in Tauragė and Klaipėda. From 2013 sea scout activity revived in Ukmergė, where the "Šventoji" unit now operates.

figures.caption: The historic Lithuanian sea scout ship "Budys".

chapters.title: Lithuanian sea scouts in the diaspora
paragraphs: As the Second World War drew to a close, most of the Lithuanians who had withdrawn from the Soviet occupation gathered at first in Germany, where, still in the refugee camps, community work, education and Lithuanian organisations began to revive. A Lithuanian maritime school was established in Flensburg, and on 10 May 1947 the Lithuanian Seamen's Union was re-established (from 1952 it was based in New York).

From 1949 most of the refugees moved overseas, mainly to the USA. After the scattered diaspora sea scout movement united under the flag of the World Lithuanian Scout Union, the most prominent centres of sea scouting were in America: in the USA — Chicago, Cleveland; in Canada — Toronto, Montreal; and also in Australia. Scout activity in the diaspora is an important means of preserving Lithuanian identity in the young generation. Bronys Stundžia, who lives in Canada, published two Lithuanian textbooks intended in part for the training of sea scouts — "Buriavimas ir jūrininkystė" (Sailing and Seamanship) in 1953 and "Burės ir varikliai" (Sails and Engines) in 1978 (reprinted in 1985 and 2000) — as well as two versions of the dictionary "Jūriniai įvardiai" (Nautical Terms); the Chicagoan Bronius Juodelis published the guidebook "Jūrinis skautavimas" (Sea Scouting) in 1976.

At present the "Nerija" girl sea scout unit is active in Lemont near Chicago in the USA; sea scout crews operate in the "Lituanica" unit and in the Toronto "Šatrija" and "Rambynas" units, and a budžiai crew operates in Melbourne, Australia.

attributes.title: Attributes
attributes.intro: The scout uniform is worn by every child or adult who scouts for any length of time. A tidy uniform is a great honour for a scout!
attributes.uniformTitle: What does a tidy uniform look like?
attributes.uniformText: For a tidy uniform you need to know where to sew the emblems, pin the badges and embroider your name. The diagram marks the place of every badge.
attributes.emblem: The emblem is made of several parts, each with its own meaning. The rue symbolises the sisterhood, and the fleur-de-lis inside it the brotherhood. The three petals of the fleur-de-lis stand for the motto "For God, Homeland and neighbour" and are joined together — the unity of scouting. The Columns of Gediminas are a symbol of Lithuania, and the anchor is a reminder not to forget safety at sea.

qa.q: What do the neckerchiefs scouts wear mean?
qa.a: Neckerchiefs mark a scout's age group. Scouts aged 6–10 wear a light blue neckerchief, 10–14 a blue one with three stripes, and 14–18 a black one with three white stripes.

qa.q: What do the three stripes on sea scout neckerchiefs mean?
qa.a: Captain Nelson sewed a white stripe onto his neckerchief after each battle won. Having won three battles, he left three white stripes — the origin of the sea scout neckerchief.

qa.q: I don't have a neckerchief. How do I get one?
qa.a: The neckerchief is received on taking the scout promise, usually given during the first year of scouting.

qa.q: Where can I get the uniform, badges and emblems?
qa.a: The uniform and all its attributes can be bought from the scout shop.
Feel free to ask the brothers and sisters at an event, or write to juru@skautai.lt.

song.title: Centennial song
song.lyrics:
Ryto rasoj plaunam kojas
Saulei nugarą atkloję
Lietų pavertę savo draugu
Dėkojam rate už tai, ką turiu.

Mazgų voratinkliai siūbuoja
Laumių žirgai nendres bučiuoja
Kaip baltos žuvėdros savo sparnus
Keliam bures į vandenis plačius.

Priedainis:
O jau....
Vakarėjant renkamės prie laužų, ų, ų,
Dieną jūra iki kelių,
O naktį ant žemės guliu, u, u
Ir sapnuoju, kad buriuoju
Aš irkluoju, prakaituoju.
Sese, broli, jūsų pakanka, kad jaustis gyvu!

Nemunu laivas aplenkia buojas.
Karta iš kartos šimtą mylių kartoja
Teleportuotis išties negali
Mes vėl su banga, o krantas toli.

Dievui, Tėvynei, Artimui, Jūrai
Žemė mėlynai alsuoja
Krantai išlydės ir pakvies į namus.
Gero vėjo ir leidžiamės į vandenis plačius...

Priedainis. x2

songbook.title: Songbook
songbook.subtitle: Sea scout songs for the campfire and the journey
songbook.intro: Song is an inseparable part of sea scout campfires, camps and hikes. Click on a song to see its words.
songbook.hymnLink: Looking for the centennial anthem "100 jūrmylių"? →
songbook.source: The songs are gathered from the "Tradicijų" booklet (Lithuanian Scouting, 2020).

songs.title: Jūreivio daina
songs.body:
Aš toks vienišas, jaunas jūreivis
mindau svetimo uosto gatves
ieškau knaipės, kur gaučiau išgerti
nes niekad nieks nemylėjo manęs.

Aš rūkau tik tai stiprią taboką
nesvarbu man prie ko tai prives
mano laimė man sukelia juoką
nes niekad nieks nemylėjo manęs.

Štai tos durys kur įpila vyno
kur dainuoja ir daužo taures
aš taip pat su visais jas daužysiu
nes niekad nieks nemylėjo manęs.

Įeinu tas duris atidaręs
ir nužvelgęs visas merginas
atsisėdu į kampą prie stalo
nes niekad nieks nemylėjo manęs.

Kai viena tu merginų prieina
pajuntu jos aistringas rankas
dar įsipilu vyno į taurę
nes niekad nieks nemylėjo manęs.

Ji paklausia „ko ieškai čia broli?
negi tau taip negaila savęs?"
atsakau jai kad nieko nenoriu
nes niekad nieks nemylėjo manęs.

Ji pasiūlo išeit pasivaikščiot
aš žinau, kur mane ji nuves
sutinku, nors gal to ir nereikia
nes niekad nieks nemylėjo manęs.

Tai jau buvo gal tūkstantį kartų
ji man taria „aš noriu tavęs"
aš jai pinigus duodu iš anksto
nes niekad nieks nemylėjo manęs.

Atsibudęs pro viešbučio langą
pamatau savo laivo bures
išeinu neištaręs nei žodžio
nes niekad nieks nemylėjo manęs.

Aš toks vienišas jaunas jūreivis
mindau svetimo uosto gatves
nežinau ar kada čia sugrįšiu
nes niekad nieks nemylėjo manęs.

songs.title: Žemyn upe
songs.body:
Mes prie Nemuno užaugę,
Mylim savo gimtą Kauną,
Nieks tikriausiai neužpyks,
Kaunas - Lietuvos širdis!

Žemyn upe, žemyn upe,
Plaukia valtis palengva -
Žemyn upe.

Kai tik vasara ateina,
Mes užtraukiam linksmą dainą,
Ir su linksma saulute -
Žemyn upe.

Nei sušilsim, nei sušalsim,
Vien tik pieną, sultis gersim.
Ir su linksma saulute -
Žemyn upe.

Mes lietuviai, mes barzdočiai,
Geriam alų gardžiai, sočiai,
Rūkom pypkę kukavinę,
Mylim savąją Tėvynę.

songs.title: Jūreivių daina
songs.body:
Ei, pirmyn, ei, drąsyn
Jūreiviai greičiau.
Neškim laiko vėliavą,
Lietuvos plačiau.

Priedainis
Nuo jūrų iki jūrų
Sklinda garsas Lietuvos,
Tai bunda Vytauto dvasia.
Į jūrą marš, marš marš marš
Į laivyną marš.

Nesitrauksim mes nuo jūros
Dievas mums padės.
Kursim, stiprinsim laivyną –
Tauta to norės. 2k

Priedainis
Nutilkit marių audros,
Plaukia Lietuvos vaikai
Mūs jaunos sesės marš į jūrą
Jūron marš marš, į jūrą marš marš,
Į laivyną marš.

Noras ir tvirta valia
Traukia jūron vis.
Į jūružę, į pasaulį –
Mūsų obalsis.  2k

Priedainis
Nuo jūrų iki jūrų
Sklinda garsas Lietuvos,
Tai bunda Vytauto dvasia.
Į jūrą marš, marš marš marš
Į laivyną marš.

songs.title: Baltos burės plazda
songs.body:
Baltos burės plazda
Bangos duokit kelią
Pasakyk, kur plaukti
Jūra motinėle

Priedainis
Tra lia lia lia lia lia lia
Tra lia lia lia lia lia
Pasakyk, kur plaukti
Jūra motinėle

Mėlyni takeliai
Mėlynos papievės,
O kuriuo pasukti –
Žino vienas Dievas

Mano laivas naujas,
Ąžuolinės sienos,
Viesulai sukaukia,
Kai aš plaukiu vienas.

Jūra motinėle
Tur dukrelių būrį
Vėjui tėvui liepiant
Visos šokti turi

Baltos burės plazda,
Bangos duokit kelią.
Neškit mane jauną,
Neškit mano dalią.

songs.title: Laivai
songs.body:
G            Em
Mes be jūros dar turime dangų
      G              Em
Ir laivus jame matom keistus.
       Am            Am
Laivai plaukia lėtai pro langus
      C        D
Ir pro būstus mūsų šaltus.
Jie nuspalvina mūsų vienatvę,
Mūsų liūdesį, dar kažką.
Tas spalvas mes paliekame gatvėj,
Jas uždengia niūri pilkuma.

        C           D
Laivus lydi lietus ir vėjas,
     G        Em
Lydi saulė ir meilė mūsų.
       C          D
Jų tiesa - mūs tiesos teisėja.
      C           D
Kaip gyventum, jei jų nebutu?

Su sparnais - be sparnų užgimę
Į savo jūrą – dangų žiūrim,
O jame mūs laivai – likimai
Visuomet su pakeltom burėm.
Jie nuspalvina mūsų vienatvę,
Mūsų liūdesį, dar kažką.
Tas spalvas mes paliekame gatvėj,
Jas uždengia niūri pilkuma.

songs.title: Jūreivis grįžta į namus
songs.body:
Jūreivis grįžta į namus
Kiria viria vim bom bom
Apsukęs žemę tris kartus
Kiria viria vim bom taip valio

Priedainis

Viktoria viktoria
Kiria viria vim bom taip valio
Viktoria viktoria
Kiria viria vim bom bom

Jis iškentė visas audras
Tik kas jo širdį čia supras

Kur jis pavargęs pailsės
Nebent mergelė pamylės

Siuvėjas jam gyvent neduos
Už kelnes vis prašys skolos

songs.title: O jūra motule
songs.body:
Prie gintaro jūros, prie marių plačių
Stovėjo jūreivis, prislėgtas minčių,
Prie jo prisiglaudus, nuleidus rankas
Graži šviesiaplaukė svajojo svajas.

Priedainis

Jūra, tavo bangos švelniai glamonėja,
Jūra, tavo pasaką seka man vėjas.
O jūra motule, kokia tu žiauri,
Kam sujungi širdis ir vėl jas skiri.

Prie gintaro jūros nuplauksime mes
Ir josios bangose pakelsim bures,
Ir klykaus žuvėdra virš marių toli,
Nebaisios bus vėtros, nei jūra žiauri.

Priedainis

Jūra, tavo bangos švelniai glamonėja,
Jūra, tavo pasaką seka man vėjas.
O jūra motule, kokia tu žiauri,
Kam sujungi širdis ir vėl jas skiri.

songs.title: Piratų daina
songs.body:
Skandinsim svetimus laivus,
Kas ne su mumis, tas prieš mus
Juoda piratų vėliava
Pergalė telydi mus.

La bela bela bela bela muzika,
Griaus muzika, griaus muzika
La bela bela bela bela muzika,
Griaus muzika, ciuriuk.

Užkas mus bet kuriuos kapuos,
Piratų net velniai bijos,
Banga, daina ir draugija
Visada telydi mus.

La bela bela bela bela muzika,
Griaus muzika, griaus muzika
La bela bela bela bela muzika,
Griaus muzika, ciuriuk.

songs.title: Parbėg laivelis
songs.body:
Parbėg laivelis nuo Venčio rago
Sidabro šotoms, auksu lioduotoms

Į tą laivelį, į tą juodąjį
Sėd ir bernelis, baltai raudonas

Eikš eikš, bernyti, eikš eikš, jaunasai
O tai mudu bėgsiv plačioms maružėms

O kad aš ėjau per Minės kiemą
Minės berneliai į man veizdėjo

O ar jūs veizdat, ar jūs neveizdat
Juk žinot patys, aš jums neteksiu

Katro norėjau, širdy turėjau
Tam aš ir daviau baltą rankelę

Dabok bernyti, kad neuždegtų
Tavo akelės mano širdelės.

songs.title: Mėnesiena
songs.body:
Mėnesionoj tarp vandens gėlių
F        G      C   G
plaukiam dviese baltu laiveliu
C            Am
Įsirėžęs traukiu irklą
F      G  C     G
visas prakaitę išmirkęs.

Tau tai gal labai gražu atrodo
O man viskas viskas viskas nusibodo
Nusibodo man kas naktį
mėnesienoj irklais plakti.

Vėl sustoju nusišypsau gudriai
Ir metu tave į tamsią upę
Vandenėlis burbuliuoja
Viso gero mylimoji.

songs.title: Prie gintaro jūros
songs.body:
Prie gintaro jūros, prie marių plačių
Stovėjo jūreivis prislėgtas minčių,
Prie jo prisiglaudus, nuleidus kasas
Graži šviesiaplaukė kalbėjo svajas.

Priedainis
Plauki su manim į jūra bekraštę
Plauki, ten lemta mums laimę surasti
Neskirs mūsų niekas, tai likimo valia
Tu vėjas, aš jūra, mes liksim drauge.

Ir ošia vis bangos, sena jų daina,
Rytoj tu išplauksi, aš liksiu viena...
Rytoj tu pranyksi melsvoj tolumoj,
Tavo sugrįžimas likimo rankoj.

Acj, jūra jūruže, tu močia žiauri,
Kam sujungei širdis ir vėl jas skiri?
Galbūt paskutinė ši mūsų naktis,
Rytoj išsiskirsim, vien meilė beliks.

songs.title: Pajūry, pajūry
songs.body:
Smėlio krantas baltuoja.
Virš bangų neramių
Baltijos vėjas skrajoja.
Mano šalis rytuose toli
Pajūryje rauda;
Pajūry, pajūry,
Smėlio kas pilį sugriovė?

O šalie mylima,
Nors tavęs nepažįstu,
Bet viena mintyse
Tavo padangėn nuklystu.
Melsvo dangaus ir laukų plačių
Visada ilgiuosi,
O šalie mylima,
Ar tave pamatysiu?

Mano šalis rytuose toli
Pajūry rauda;
Pajūry, pajūry,
Smėlio kas pilį sugriovė?

songs.title: Baltijos vėjelis
songs.body:
Baltijos vėjelis tilsta jau.
Sesutė laivelį sups ramiau.
Tieskime bures, plaukim į jūrą,
Prie žvejų arčiau.

Palauki broleli, dar palauk
Toli į jūružę dar neplauk.
Ten prie Palangos didelės bangos,
Broli, aš bijau.

Priedainis:

Plaukia laivelis, supas ramiai.
Tra lia lia lia lia lia lia supas ramiai.
Skamba dainelė, ošia šilai
Tra lia lia lia lia lial ia lia ošia šilai.
Saulutė leidžias vakaruose.
Tra lia lia lia lia lia lia vakaruose.
Spinduliai maudos jau bangose,
Tra lia lia lia lia lia lia jau bangose.

Grįžkim grįžkim, broluželi,
Gamta jau kviečia poilsio.
Jau žvaigždutėm dangus nusėtas,
Laikas ir mum sapnais grožėtis

songs.title: Vai linksmai plaukia
songs.body:
Vai linksmai plaukia
Geltons laivelis
Ant jūružių marelių.    2k

Vai šipor šipor,
Jauns šiporėli*,
Įlipki į mastelį**.

Ten tu matysi
Kopų kalnely
Naują rūtų darželį.

Tame daržely,
Tame naujajam,
Stovi jauna mergelė.

Kai puč vėjelis,
Tai bėg laivelis,
Linksma žvejų mergelė.

Nepuč vėjelis
Nebėg laivelis,
Liūdna žvejų mergelė.

songs.title: Ūžia jūružė
songs.body:
Ūžia jūružė
Bangos krantą
plauna,
Vėjas įtūžęs
Vis blaškos į duris

Žvejo laivelį
Sūkuriai gramzdina
Vėjas nuo stiebo
Nudraskė jau bures.

Kopų grytelėj
Mirga žiburėlis.
Meldžias už tėvą.

songs.title: Turiu laivelį mažą
songs.body:
Turiu laivelį mažą
Plonų plonų lentučių,
Jį man padarė tėtis,
Kad jūrininku būčiau.

Buvau Jonelis mažas,
Dabar jau didis Jonas.
Ir ant širdies taip gera,
Kad aš jau kapitonas.

Einu krantu smėlėtu,
Bangelės tyliai šnara.
Ei,plaukite žuvelės,
Ir duokit laivui kelią.

songs.title: Jūrų skautų daina „Ritas bangos“
songs.body:
Ritas bangos, plazda burės,
Plevėsuoja lelija,
Jaunus vėjus pasikinkius,
Plaukia jūrų skautija.

Marš, marš marš....
Ei valio, valio jūruže
Ei valio, valio maruže
Jaunus vėjus pasikinkius,
Plaukia jūrų skautija

Nors ir žydrūs vandenynai,
Bet mums moja Baltija.
Kur Jūratė kelia tiesia
Juosta gintaro plačia.

Marš, marš marš....
Ei valio, valio jūruže
Ei valio, valio maruže
Kur Jūratė kelia tiesia
Juosta gintaro plačia.

Kilkit bangos, vėjai neškit
Mūsų dvasią ant sparnų.
Baltą vytį, šiaurės žvaigždę
Neišleidžiam iš akių.

Marš, marš marš....
Ei valio, valio jūruže
Ei valio, valio maruže
Baltą vytį, šiaurės žvaigždę
Neišleidžiam iš akių.

oaths.title: Promises and prayer
oaths.subtitle: The pledges taken by sea scouts
oaths.intro: Sea scouts have a tradition of the "wet" promise. Promises are most often taken outdoors by the water, before the sea scout or Lithuanian flags. Before the promise is taken the ends of the neckerchief are wetted, and the one who has just taken the promise is often christened by being dipped in the water.

items.text: „Aš pasižadu stengtis mylėti Dievą, Tėvynę, Jūrą, vykdyti jaunesniųjų skautų priesakus ir kasdien padaryti gerą darbelį"
items.note: The promise is most often taken before 12.00. It is public and may be given during a parade or another event.

items.text: „Brangindamas (-a) savo garbę, aš pasižadu stengtis tarnauti Dievui ir Tėvynei, mylėti Jūrą, padėti Artimui ir vykdyti skautų (-čių) priesakus."
items.note: The promise is most often taken before the evening parade. It is public and may be given during a parade or another event.

items.text: „Brangindamas (-a) savo garbę, aš pasižadu stengtis tarnauti Dievui ir Tėvynei, mylėti Jūrą, padėti Artimui, vykdyti skautų (-čių) priesakus ir būti pavyzdžiu jaunesniesiems skautams."
items.note: The promise is most often taken before 24.00. It is not public; by established tradition the promise is organised by the water or on the water.

items.text: „Brangindamas (-a) savo garbę aš pasižadu stengtis tarnauti Dievui ir Tėvynei, mylėti Jūrą, padėti Artimui, vykdyti skautų (-čių) priesakus ir visomis jėgomis dirbti skautijos gerovei."

„Brangindamas(-a) savo garbę, aš pasižadu stengtis tarnauti Dievui, Tėvynei ir Artimui, vykdyti skautų (-čių) priesakus ir visomis jėgomis dirbti Lietuvos skautijos gerovei ir Lietuvos jūrinei idėjai."

prayer.title: The Sea Scout prayer
prayer.intro: Sea scouts have their own prayer. This prayer has come down to us from the interwar period, when sea scouting was at its height. River sailings, lake activities and trips out to sea were organised, and so a prayer was composed to be dedicated to the sea scouts. The prayer was passed on by Koleta Chmieliauskaitė, an active interwar sea scout who in 1933, together with her father, her brother and the scout B. Rėkus, sailed in two kayaks from Obeliai across Lake Sartai, along the Šventoji and the Neris to Kaunas in 10 days. In all they covered 266 kilometres. This sister was a pioneer of the girl sea scouts in Lithuania.
prayer.text:
Visagalis Dieve, kūrėjau visatos,
kurio klauso vėjai ir bangos,
kuris nustatai saulės ir žvaigždžių kelius,
į tave mes, jūrų skautai, kreipiamės.
Stiprink mus tikėjime,
duok dvasios ramybės, ryžto ir jėgos.
Globok mūsų kraštą ir artimuosius,
laimink mūsų žygius ir savo apvaizda saugoki,
kad jaunos krūtinės, tvirtesnės už laivo medį,
atlaikytu bangų puolimą ir
tamsią naktį, per rūką ir audras,
mūsų laivai nepalūžusiais stiebais,
laimingai pasiektų uostą. Amen
